name: MICROVISION, INC.
summary: MicroVision, Inc. develops lidar-based perception solutions for automotive, industrial, and security & defense markets. Founded in 1993 and headquartered in Redmond, Washington, the company offers a portfolio of solid-state lidar sensors including MOVIA, MAVIN, IRIS, HALO, and Scantinel FMCW lidar, along with perception and validation software. Solutions enable ADAS, autonomous driving, robotics, warehouse automation, agriculture, mining, and defense applications. Engineering teams operate in the U.S. and Germany.

products.name: MOVIA
products.description: Flash-based short- to mid-range solid-state lidar sensor for automotive and industrial markets.

products.name: MAVIN
products.description: MEMS-based 905nm long-range solid-state lidar sensor capable of small object detection.

products.name: IRIS
products.description: 1550nm time-of-flight long-range lidar sensor, automotive-grade, acquired from Luminar Technologies.

products.name: HALO
products.description: Next-generation 1550nm long-range lidar based on IRIS architecture with improved performance and reduced size and cost.

products.name: Scantinel FMCW Lidar
products.description: 1550nm frequency-modulated continuous wave lidar-on-chip solution for long-range applications including commercial vehicles.

products.name: SENTINEL
products.description: Full-stack software platform under development supporting safety and autonomy for passenger vehicles and commercial trucks.

products.name: Perception Software
products.description: Automotive-qualified software stack with localization, fusion, and customer-specific application features.

products.name: MOSAIK
products.description: Tool for validating vehicle sensors for ADAS and autonomous driving applications; investment reduced in 2024.

partnerships.description: Direct marketing and co-development relationships for lidar sensors and software integration.
Partnerships to supply integrated lidar hardware and software solutions for serial production.
Partnerships to support MicroVision solutions on compute platforms.
Issued senior secured convertible notes totaling up to $75M in October 2024 and additional agreements in 2025 and 2026.
